mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 8 Series 840i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 840i engine typically costs £2,500–£7,500 supply only depending on generation, with the E31 V8s lower and the B58 higher. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your 840i.

It depends which generation your 840i is. The E31 M62 is the most reliable V8 with timing chain guides as its main weakness. The B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. The E31 M60 is the most maintenance‑sensitive. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 840i engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: E31 M60: Nikasil liner degradation. E31 M62: timing chain guide failure. G14/G15/G16 B58: coolant loss (water pump, expansion cap), PCV valve failure, and oil filter housing leaks. Each generation has its own character and watchpoints.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an 840i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. An E31 840i is a classic — values range from £8,000–£30,000, making replacement viable in many cases. A G14/G15/G16 840i is a £25,000–£60,000 vehicle — a £5,000–£7,000 recon is 8–28% of the car's value, making replacement a clear decision. Evaluate carefully based on your car's generation and condition.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 840i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 840i covers M60 (E31 1993–1996), M62 (E31 1996–1999), B58B30M1 (G14/G15/G16 2018–2022), and B58B30C (G14/G15/G16 LCI 2022+). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm which one you have for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the common failures on an 840i?

acceptedAnswer.text: On E31 M60: use low‑sulphur fuel and have compression tested periodically. On E31 M62: timing chain guides replacement at 100,000 miles is recommended. On B58: regular coolant system checks, BMW‑spec oil changes, and PCV system inspections are the most effective prevention. At the first sign of rattle, coolant loss or rough running, book an inspection immediately.

problems.title: Nikasil Cylinder Liner Degradation (M60, E31 1993–1996)
problems.symptoms: Misfires, rough idle, loss of compression, increased oil consumption, engine warning light.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if misfires occur

problems.recommendation: The M60 V8's Nikasil cylinder liners can degrade when exposed to high‑sulphur fuel. If you experience misfires or rough running, have a compression test performed immediately. Many early M60 engines have been replaced; if yours hasn't, budget for a bottom‑end rebuild or replacement.

problems.title: Timing Chain Guide Failure (M62, E31 1996–1999)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking, engine warning light, loss of power.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught before chain jumps)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M62's timing chain guides are the defining weakness — they become brittle and can shatter, causing the chain to jump. If you hear a rattling noise on cold start, have the guides inspected immediately. Regular oil changes with the correct spec are essential. Many owners replace the guides preventatively around 100,000 miles.

problems.title: Coolant Loss (B58, G14/G15/G16 2018+)
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning light, sudden drop in coolant level, overheating, visible coolant puddles under the car.
problems.repairCost: £400–£900 (water pump or expansion tank/cap replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant levels drop

problems.recommendation: B58 coolant loss is one of the most frequently reported issues. The electric water pump can fail, and the expansion tank cap may not seal properly. If you notice the coolant level dropping, have the cooling system pressure‑tested immediately. Replacing a failing water pump preventatively is far cheaper than the overheating damage it can cause.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 840i? Check These First
buyIf: * E31 M60: Nikasil liners replaced or compression test passed
* E31 M62: timing chain guides replaced or inspected
* G14/G15/G16 B58: cooling system maintained with recent water pump work
* Full service history with BMW-spec oil for the correct era
avoidIf: * E31 M60: misfires or rough running — Nikasil may be degrading — walk away
* E31 M62: cold‑start rattle — timing chain guides may be failing
* G14/G15/G16 B58: coolant loss with no diagnosis — walk away
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — potentially expensive

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 840i badge is a journey through BMW grand touring history — from the classic E31 V8 to the modern G14/G15/G16 B58 straight‑six. The E31 M62 is the most reliable V8; the B58 is the most refined and reliable overall. The M60 is the most maintenance‑sensitive. Buy on generation‑specific history — and never ignore a cold‑start rattle or coolant loss.

cta.label: → Compare 840i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 8 Series 840i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling (M62 timing chain guides)
* Misfires (E31 M60) — Check for rough running; Nikasil liner degradation indicator
* Coolant Level (B58) — Check for unexplained loss; water pump may be failing
* Service History — including which generation (E31, G14/G15/G16 pre‑LCI or LCI) and confirmed maintenance
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* PCV Valve (B58) — Check for rough idle; PCV failure indicator
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures
